
::: {.paper-table}
**Table 22. B.4 Cardiovascular and metabolic — a class of real, modest-to-large effects**

| Intervention | Outcome | Effect | Source |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Statins, secondary prevention | CV mortality, MI, stroke | Relative risk reduction ~25–30%; NNT 19–21 over 10 years | CTT Collaboration |
| Antihypertensives, per 10 mmHg SBP | Major CV events | RR=0.80; consistent across drug classes | Ettehad et al. [@ettehad2016] |
| SGLT2 inhibitors (empagliflozin) | CV death in T2D with CV disease | HR=0.62 | EMPA-REG OUTCOME 2015 |
| SGLT2 inhibitors (dapagliflozin) | Heart failure hospitalization / CV death | HR=0.74 | DAPA-HF 2019 |
| PCSK9 inhibitors | LDL-C lowering | ~60% additional reduction on top of statin | FOURIER, ODYSSEY |
| Aspirin in acute MI | Vascular mortality | 23% RRR | ISIS-2 1988 |
| Warfarin / DOACs in AF | Stroke prevention | ~65% RRR | AFASAK, multiple pivotal trials |
| Semaglutide 2.4mg (obesity) | Weight loss | ~15% body weight reduction vs. ~2.5% placebo | STEP trials |
| Tirzepatide 15mg (obesity) | Weight loss | ~21% body weight reduction | SURMOUNT-1 |
:::
